capsules.summary: Acquisition and cleanup must be structurally coupled.
Separate code from data in query construction.
Make valid operation sequences explicit and machine-checkable.
Every dangerous sink or privilege boundary carries its own validation contract.
TLS certificate verification must remain enabled on all outbound connections.
Dynamic code execution primitives must be replaced with safe alternatives.

ruby: Kernel#send with whitelist instead of eval

capsules.summary: Placeholder markers (TODO/FIXME) are forbidden in production artifacts.
Function default arguments must not be mutable objects — they persist across all calls.

python: def f(x=None): if x is None: x = []

capsules.summary: Shell commands must use explicit argument lists, not shell=True with string interpolation.
File permission checks must be atomic with subsequent operations. No TOCTOU gaps.

python: os.open with O_NOFOLLOW or pathlib atomic operations

capsules.summary: User-supplied URLs must have host validation before network requests.

javascript: validate host against allowlist before fetch/axios
python: check parsed URL netloc against denylist before requests.get

capsules.summary: Security tokens must use cryptographically secure random number generators.
Financial calculations must use fixed-point or decimal arithmetic, not float.
Unsafe memory operations must verify alignment and size before raw pointer operations.

rust: check size_of::<T>() and align_of::<T>() before raw cast
c: assert len >= sizeof(Header) before pointer cast
cpp: use reinterpret_cast with static_assert on alignment

capsules.summary: Async check-act sequences on shared state must be wrapped in atomic transactions.

javascript: SELECT FOR UPDATE or compare-and-swap atomic operations
python: SELECT FOR UPDATE within transaction context

capsules.summary: Taint from user input must not reach SQL sinks through function call chains.

python: parameterize ALL query components including table/column names

capsules.summary: Whitelist-guarded setters must be the only write path to sensitive attributes.

python: route all writes through guarded setter; remove unguarded setattr paths

capsules.summary: Classes must not have circular initialization dependencies.

python: use lazy init or dependency injection; never call mediator in __init__

capsules.summary: Security token comparison must be constant-time to prevent timing oracles.
Path sanitization must cover all encoding variants, not just literal '..'.

python: unquote + realpath + startswith(base) — in that order

capsules.summary: Regular expressions must not contain nested quantifiers that cause exponential backtracking.

python: use re2 library or rewrite pattern to eliminate nesting

capsules.summary: MD5 and SHA1 are cryptographically broken and must not be used for security purposes.

python: bcrypt.hashpw / passlib.hash.argon2 for passwords; hashlib.sha256+ for digests

capsules.summary: Deserialization of user-supplied data using pickle/yaml.load executes arbitrary code.

python: json.loads for structured data; yaml.safe_load if YAML required

capsules.summary: getattr() with user-controlled attribute name is eval in disguise.
verify=False in default argument propagates insecure TLS to all callers.
User input in template strings enables server-side template injection (SSTI).
redirect() with user-supplied URL enables phishing and OAuth token theft.
User input in XPATH, LDAP, or XML queries enables structured query injection.
setattr() over user-controlled dict without allowlist enables mass assignment attacks.
printf with user-controlled format string enables arbitrary memory read/write.
Integer multiplication in malloc/calloc can overflow, causing under-allocation.
Goroutine with channel send but no send on error path — goroutine leaks.
Object.keys() iteration without constructor guard enables prototype pollution.
Class.forName() or getDeclaredMethod() with user-controlled arguments enables arbitrary class loading.
base64-decoded JSON trusted for role/admin without signature verification — attacker fabricates identity claims.
JWT decode must specify algorithms= to prevent algorithm confusion attacks.
Internal network calls must use HTTPS, not cleartext HTTP.
Resource access via externally-supplied ID must enforce object-level ownership. Prevents IDOR — user can reach the action but may not own the object.
Database queries accepting external IDs must scope results to the authenticated user to prevent horizontal privilege escalation.
XML parsers that expand external entities allow XXE attacks — read local files, SSRF, DoS
Hardcoded credentials in source are extractable from binaries, version control, and memory dumps
Cookies without HttpOnly/Secure/SameSite expose sessions to XSS, network sniffing, and CSRF
CORS wildcard (*) allows any origin to read responses; combined with credentials this is critical
User input in HTTP headers enables CRLF injection, response splitting, and cookie injection
Resource access by external ID without ownership check enables IDOR — any user can access any record
tempfile.mktemp is a TOCTOU race — use tempfile.mkstemp or NamedTemporaryFile
ssl._create_unverified_context disables certificate verification — susceptible to MITM
telnetlib/ftplib transmit credentials in cleartext — use SSH/SFTP
pycrypto/Crypto library has known vulnerabilities — use cryptography package
os.access() + open() is a TOCTOU race — check and act must be atomic
django mark_safe() bypasses template escaping — stored XSS if user data reaches it
ORM .raw() and .extra() with user input bypasses parameterization
marshal.loads can execute arbitrary code on untrusted data
yaml.load() without Loader executes arbitrary Python via YAML tags
subprocess with shell=True and string arg enables shell injection via metacharacters
SSLv2/v3/TLS1.0/1.1 are cryptographically broken — POODLE, BEAST, DROWN
NoSQL operator injection via user-controlled $where/$ne/$gt in MongoDB queries
Bidirectional Unicode control characters disguise malicious logic (CVE-2021-42574)
